nodes.context: Polish-American organized crime group active in the US since the 1920s, involved in drug trafficking, racketeering, and other crimes.
Italian-American crime syndicate based in Chicago, led by Al Capone during Prohibition.
Mostly Irish-American gang with Polish presence, operated in Chicago during Prohibition.
Polish-American gang operating in Philadelphia, involved in drug trafficking, bookmaking, and loansharking.
Polish criminal organization based in Greenpoint, Brooklyn, involved in gunrunning, armed robbery, and drug trafficking.

edges.context: During Prohibition, Polish-American gangsters Joseph Saltis and Jake Guzik allied with Al Capone's Chicago Outfit.
The North Side Gang had a large Polish presence, including Hymie Weiss of Polish heritage.
The Kielbasa Posse does not have a good relationship with the K&A Gang, which controls most of Northeast Philly.
